summary: xAI has the lowest cost-per-token due to superior data center construction and GPU utilization
summary_extended: Baker argues that xAI (Elon Musk's AI company) has achieved the lowest cost-per-token in the AI industry due to what he describes as a 'superhuman' approach to data center construction and GPU utilization. Specifically, he claims xAI builds data centers faster and at lower cost than competitors, and runs its GPUs at higher utilization rates than peers, creating a structural cost advantage in AI inference and training. This cost advantage, if sustained, would allow xAI to undercut competitors on pricing or generate superior margins as AI model usage scales. However, xAI is a private company with no US-listed equity, meaning there is no directly investable public market instrument tied to this thesis. Baker presents this as a factual structural claim based on his own analysis, with no third-party corroboration cited, limiting actionability for public market investors.
detail: Baker claims xAI builds data centers faster and at lower cost than peers, runs GPUs at higher utilization, and therefore has the lowest cost per token in the industry. This is a structural cost advantage.
confidence_evidence: Stated as a factual claim but sourced from Baker's own analysis; no third-party corroboration cited
quote_fragment: Xxii has the lowest cost per token because they build data centers in a superhuman way
